column2: #Make a list of the paths of files so that R can import with read_csv
#Import fluoroprobe data from mussel feeding exp
names_to = 'category', values_to = 'chla') #This step will help later in rate calculations.
#Average the plate reader technical replicates. Using across() saves us from doing the renaming ourselves
dplyr::summarise(across(c(chla, time), list(avg = mean, sd = sd), .names = "{.fn}_{.col}")) |>
#There was a lag between T0 and T1 because of waiting for mussels to open. This corrects that issue.
avg_time = ifelse(Timepoint == 'TF', avg_time[avg_time == max(avg_time[avg_time != max(avg_time)])], avg_time),
source = case_when(Timepoint == "TF" ~ "Mixed", #Water column is during the experiment while mixed is the final reading.
